title: MEETING FAMILIES WHERE THEY ARE AT: IMPLEMENTING A CULTURALLY SENSITIVE COMMUNICATION PROTOCOL TO IMPROVE SCHOOL FAMILY PARTNERSHIPS
author: Nguyen-Kung, Thao
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This improvement study examined how culturally sensitive communication (CSC), supported by technology, can strengthen school–family partnerships (SFP) in diverse schools. Traditional communication methods such as newsletters or automated calls have failed to meet the needs of culturally and linguistically diverse (CLD) families, contributing to persistent inequities in attendance, achievement, and student well-being. Using improvement science and equity-centered leadership frameworks, this study tested a schoolwide CSC protocol developed and was implemented by secondary school staff. The protocol incorporated digital platforms with real-time translation, centralized messaging, and culturally responsive outreach strategies. Through iterative plan-do-study-act (PDSA) cycles, school staff participants adapted the protocol to address site-specific barriers including chronic absenteeism, disengagement, and inconsistent communication.

Using improvement science and equity-centered leadership frameworks, this study tested a schoolwide CSC protocol developed and was implemented by secondary school staff. The protocol incorporated digital platforms with real-time translation, centralized messaging, and culturally responsive outreach strategies. Through iterative plan-do-study-act (PDSA) cycles, school staff participants adapted the protocol to address site-specific barriers including chronic absenteeism, disengagement, and inconsistent communication.
